Template # 1 

Market’s Search for Meaning



Ry Schwartz & Copyhackers

2020



Template # 1

About “Market’s Search for Meaning”

What we’re “coaching”:

Where to use it:

Exploratory Questions:

Themeplate

See it in action:

Example 1: Holistic Health Space

Example 2: Marketing software (Affiliate Email for Derek Halpern’s Zippy Courses)







About “Market’s Search for Meaning” 



This is one of my favorite email templates to use early in a sales sequence, or even as a standalone sales email -- especially when I know my ideal prospect has experienced shame or embarrassment around previous “missteps” related to the problem my product solves. 



It’s powerful because most of our well-targeted prospects who don’t end up buying,  don’t even know why they didn’t buy. Instead, they subconsciously disqualify themselves based on old cognitive biases, shameful experiences, negative self-imaging, etc. 



By reframing those negative self-perceptions, and giving them a chance to derive new positive meaning through us and our offer - we can RE-QUALIFY many of our lost customers. 

What we’re “coaching”:



Self Forgiveness/Acceptance (“it’s not your fault”, “it was the best thing at the time”)

Clarity and understanding around previous behavior

Positive self-image as it relates to those previously ‘negative” events/actions

A chance to stay consistent with that new meaning (through our offer). 



Where to use it:

As a standalone sales email

An early sales follow up after a conversion event (ie. webinar / launch announcement / presentation)

Exploratory Questions:



What “bad” behavior or actions, in relation to what you’re selling, might your prospect feel ashamed about?



What external thing (a person, common belief, etc.) can we deflect some of the blame to? 



How was that “bad” action in fact positive (or the best action possible at the time)? 



What additional pain may it have saved them from?



How did it prepare or empower them for this moment? 

	





Themeplate



[tell story about their experience of that difficult / shameful trait/action/behavior]

[optionally deflect the blame to something external] 

[bridge into WHY it actually wasn’t their fault]

[how was that thing actually a good thing - a positive reframe to open up the meaning loop]

[but now there’s a better (more appropriate) way - closing the meaning loop]

[tying the meaning loop to your offer] 

[overcome surface level objections through your key features/benefits]



See it in action: 

Example 1: Holistic Health Space 



SUBJECT: 

If you could go back in time, would you change *this* moment? 



BODY:



There was a moment.



A seemingly innocent one. 



1 month, 1 year, or even 10 years ago.



When you sat in an office. Hands clasped together. Posture bent in forward.



A moment of suspended anticipation when your well-intentioned (yet mis-informed) Doctor pulled out a prescription pad.



You watched eagerly as he mindlessly scribbled a name on a piece of paper.



Prozac. Paxil. Zoloft.



A moment that lasted all of three seconds. 



The sound of your Doctor ripping the page from the pad probably still echoes deep in your memory.



The reassuring smile that coaxed you into taking it from his hand. 



And the instant sense of relief you experienced while pocketing it.



The promise of hope.



The promise that you could “beat” your depression with a cocktail of chemicals more foreign to you and your body than anything you could ever have fathomed at the time. 



Perhaps in the back of your mind, there was a tiny voice saying:



“Do I really want to depend on this pill for the rest of my life?”



Yet the quick-fix relief and trust in the medical system was more than enough to sweep aside any sense of doubt. 



And it was this overwhelming sense of relief (and the system that reinforced it)...



… that blinded you to the sheer fact that you had just pocketed a life-sentence. 



Fortunately, it’s also a reversible one. 



And it’s important to remind yourself that this critical *moment* was not your fault. 



For myself (and the hundreds of women I’ve treated)....



We would have given anything to travel back to this moment and make a more informed choice. 



A more courageous one.



Yet we’re also self-forgiving to the fact that we made the choice we truly believed was best for us at the time.



In many cases, the ONLY choice available to us. 



And in doing so… let go of any guilt, shame, and yes, even anger in admitting what we may have put our minds and bodies through with that decision. 



I can’t offer you a re-do, <first name>.



But I can offer you a recovery. 



Drug-free. Side-Effect free. 



The X system is the exact same self-healing program that’s been behind hundreds of recoveries (of even the most skeptical women who believed they would never be able to “get off the meds”). 



And for the 245 women who are already to say YES to the X beta group, a full return to mind and body health is just a few short weeks away.



If you'd like to join them… 



That same re-set is here waiting for you. 



All you have to do is lean in with the same anticipation you did when you first took that prescription paper.



Except this time, with the reassurance that what you’ll experience isn’t an artificial quick-fix…



… but a lifelong relationship with your most vibrant, radiant, joyful self.



So if you’re ready for that, <first name>



Let’s revoke your “life sentence”.



And reclaim your wholeness. 



In love and healing,



(sign off) 



Example 2: Marketing software (Affiliate Email for Derek Halpern’s Zippy Courses) 



Subject:

Fire this business coach… 

If your business coach ever says this…

Entrepreneurial “Shaming” at it’s worse (GRR!) 

“stop procrastinating” (GRRR) 



Body:



“Stop procrastinating”



Ugh.



It’s like the low-hanging fruit that a rookie business coach will call you out on whenever your big dreams (ie. creating a profitable online course) get put on hold. 



And personally, I can’t stand it. 



It’s condescending. 



Presumptuous. 



Downright degrading.



And worse of all… 



A million miles removed from the truth. 



Cause the thing is… procrastination isn’t a “cause”...



It’s an “effect” of not having the confidence to go “all in” on your project.



Often a legitimate, protective one that saves you and your family from: 



…. needless turmoil and overwhelm. 



…. endless nights trying to find the one tech guy who actually gets what you’re trying to build, and won’t charge half your annual income to do it. 



…  and a quickly dissolving bank account without the eventual payoff or end product to show for it (which makes for awkward convos with the fam). 



So I’m here to clear the air.



Particularly when it comes to building your time-leveraged and profitable online course.



It’s one thing to have an evernote (or google doc) stacked with ideas, content, lessons, modules, etc…



But a whole other to actually feel confident about putting it out in the world. 



And unlike what the finger-pointing business coach might tell you… 



You haven’t PROCRASTINATED in getting your amazing course out of your head and into the world…



You haven’t chosen to “play small”



And you definitely don’t have any “limiting beliefs” around the massive impact and exciting new revenue streams available to you. 



You’ve simply (and appropriately) waited it out for a better time…



One where you can build it with confidence, knowing that you’ll be spared from the same tech horror stories, and customer-repelling blunders that have plagued dozens of your entrepreneurial peers. 



Cause you’ve heard the horror stories.



And even though you’ve repeatedly told yourself:



“That won’t happen to me”



A more rational part of you knows that unless you take an actual step in protecting yourself from those same tech failures…



You’ll be destined to be just another angry voice in tech-haters anonymous.



With a half-finished online course and a fully-emptied bank account. 



So THAT is what has kept you “procrastinating” on your online course. 



What has kept you from serving your market with your well-honed expertise and unique way of doing things. 



And from having a time-leveraged asset that frees you from the time for money trap while growing your revenue to exciting new levels.



And while ZIppy Courses does SO much amazing stuff to remove those barriers… 



What it really does better than anything I’ve ever seen is…  



It gives you permission to hit play. 



(and to fire the coach who EVER had the nerve to call you lazy).



Forever In your corner, 



Amy



 PS. Here are 3 amazing ways that Zippy Courses ends procrastination:



No list to sell your course to? Not confident in your idea? With Zippy you can create short “lead magnet” courses in minutes... and then use Derek’s high-converting landing page template to generate leads (and beta testers) before building your BIG course. 



Creativity stifled? An expert or skilled professional WITHOUT an easy-to-use course building platform (like Zippy Courses) is like a world-class author without a pen. 



	It’s damaging to our entrepreneurial juices when we secretly know 

that even if we come up with the best course ideas (and content) we won’t have the skills or software to get it out to the world in a seamless, powerful and pain-free way. 



ZIppy Courses gives you back the confidence to create. 



      3. Ok great. But how will I sell the thing? What’s the use in 

creating something if no one will buy it, right? 



You’re an entrepreneur. I don’t expect you to invest time and 

energy in creating a course simply for the “joy of the journey”. 



And neither does Derek.



That’s why Zippy Courses also comes with a sales page template 

optimized specifically for selling an online course.



Plus, with the hundreds (or thousands) you’ll save in freelance 

fees or a pricier solution -- you can reinvest part of those savings 

into an ad campaign that drives more buyers into your course. 



Save 50% on Zippy Courses (valid until 8/23)
